---
name: design-review
description: Guide for reviewing design of software system or modules
---

To review the design of software systems or modules, follow this process:

1. Understand the requirements by reading the requirement spec provided by the user in the directory $0.
2. Identify the issues that are not explained precisely in the requirement spec, and ask the user questions about them to get a perfect understanding.
3. Review the design created by the user or another agent, and provide constructive feedback about the issues with the design. When doing review, focus on:
  - the correctness and completeness of the design against the requirements
  - the quality of the design, such as maintainability, readability, scalability, security, performance, and user experience.
4. Never provide feedback about the strength of the design. You only provide constructive criticism. Prioritize the issues if possible.
